heading: Studies and reports; examination and audit
label: 4716 Studies and reports; examination and audit
notes: Amendments2008—Subsec. (e). substituted “Federal Housing Finance Agency” for “Federal Housing Finance Board”.Pub. L. 110–289
amendments: Amendments2008—Subsec. (e). substituted “Federal Housing Finance Agency” for “Federal Housing Finance Board”.Pub. L. 110–289

children.heading: Annual report by Fund
children.label: a Annual report by Fund
children.text: The Fund shall conduct an annual evaluation of the activities carried out by the Fund and the community development financial institutions and other organizations assisted pursuant to this subchapter, and shall submit a report of its findings to the President and the Congress not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year of the Fund. The report shall include financial statements audited in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: Optional studies
children.label: b Optional studies
children.text: The Fund may conduct such studies as the Fund determines necessary to further the purpose of this subchapter and to facilitate investment in distressed communities. The findings of any studies conducted pursuant to this subsection shall be included in the report required by subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: identify barriers to private financing on such lands; and

children.label: B
children.text: identify the impact of such barriers on access to capital and credit for Native American populations.

children.label: A
children.text: contains the findings of the study conducted under paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: recommends any necessary statutory and regulatory changes to existing Federal programs; and

children.label: C
children.text: makes policy recommendations for community development financial institutions, insured depository institutions, secondary market institutions, and other private sector capital institutions to better serve such populations.

children.heading: Investment, governance, and role of Fund
children.label: d Investment, governance, and role of Fund
children.text: Thirty months after the appointment and qualification of the Administrator, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the President and the Congress a study evaluating the structure, governance, and performance of the Fund.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: e Consultation
children.text: In the conduct of the studies required under this section, the Fund shall consult, as appropriate, with the Comptroller of the Currency, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Federal Housing Finance Agency, the Farm Credit Administration, the Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision, the National Credit Union Administration Board, Indian tribal governments, community reinvestment organizations, civil rights organizations, consumer organizations, financial organizations, and such representatives of agencies or other persons, at the discretion of the Fund.

children.heading: Examination and audit
children.label: f Examination and audit
children.text: The financial statements of the Fund shall be audited in accordance with , except that audits required by section 9105(a) of such title shall be performed annually.section 9105 of title 31
